blocks.content: She looked up when she heard the bikes.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Jenkins.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Jones.
He did a slow circle in the parking lot, because riding away immediately would have been too obvious.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You almost killed Mrs. Dupont's azaleas.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: She saw that?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Half the street saw that.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: The half that matters or the half that doesn't?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: The half with eyes.
She gave him the ceiling fan look — the one that meant she was deciding whether the situation was salvageable. Savannah leaned over and said something in her ear. Daisy pressed her lips together.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: What.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Nothing.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: What did she say.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: She said the azaleas were probably fine.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: That is not what I said.
Eli had already pulled up and was inspecting the paper cup in Daisy's hand.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Is that the cherry slush? You got the cherry slush without me?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: The Tasty Freeze is three blocks from here, Eli.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: And yet.

Daisy handed him the rest of it without being asked. Eli accepted it like this was his right, which, in some sense, it was. Kit watched her do it — just gave it over, like it cost nothing — and filed that somewhere without knowing he was doing it.
He pulled his bike up alongside the wall.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: What are y'all doing today.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We were going to the library.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: We are not going to the library.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I was considering the library.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You were not going to the library.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You don't know what I was going to do.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: It's eighty-four degrees and you're sitting on a wall eating a slush. You were not going to the library.
Daisy considered this for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: What are you doing.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Old water tower.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: No.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You haven't heard the full pitch yet.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Kit. It's condemned.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Condemned means the town doesn't want to deal with liability. The rungs are solid. You can see the whole town from up there.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: How do you know the rungs are solid if you've never been up?
A brief pause.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I believe they are solid.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: That is not the same thing.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I've seen them from the fence. They look pretty solid.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Eli, you thought the Henderson barn had horses in it.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I still think that's possible.
Savannah was already pulling her hair back into a ponytail, which was essentially agreement. Daisy saw it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Savannah.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: I'm just pulling my hair back.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: In preparation for climbing a condemned water tower.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: I make no promises.
Daisy looked at the three of them. Then at her empty paper cup.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: If one of us gets tetanus, I want it on record that I said rusted rungs.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Noted. For the record.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I'm serious, Kit.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I know. It's in the record.
She got on her bike.
He didn't smile about it until he was a few feet ahead of her, where she couldn't see.
